title: Schefflera maguireorum
taxonomy: Schefflera maguireorum is classified within the family Araliaceae and is a species of flowering plant. It is native to Queensland, Australia. Schefflera maguireorum is further classified within the genus Schefflera, which contains approximately 600 species. Within the genus, Schefflera maguireorum is placed in the section Actinophylla, characterized by its unique leaf morphology. The species is also sometimes referred to as a member of the family Umbelliferae, although this classification is now considered outdated.

However, more studies are needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human use. The plant's leaves contain various bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which may contribute to its potential medicinal properties. Further research is necessary to fully understand the medicinal uses of Schefflera maguireorum.
